title: Provide Generous Targets and Non-Gesture Alternatives
summary: Build and verify css touch targets in shift action accidental-activation review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css touch targets from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css touch targets without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 01 · Inspect the shift action accidental-activation review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves css touch targets when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: shift action accidental-activation review.

content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

Provide Generous Targets and Non-Gesture Alternatives checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Shift action accidental-activation review case 1: small icons are usable when their SVG graphic is visually sharp.

Shift action accidental-activation review case 1: size the interactive area rather than only the glyph, separate adjacent targets, and provide simple pointer and keyboard alternatives to complex gestures.
Shift action accidental-activation review case 1: make icons eight pixels wide and add a tooltip for users who miss them.

steps.title: 04 · Read the shift action accidental-activation review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css touch targets when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: shift action accidental-activation review.
steps.why: Provide Generous Targets and Non-Gesture Alternatives makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in shift action accidental-activation review.

content.text: Target usability depends on clickable area, spacing, nearby targets, posture, tremor, screen size, zoom, and input precision—not SVG sharpness. Controls need generous inline and block area, visible boundaries, and separation. Path-based or multipoint gestures need simple alternatives, and orientation should not be locked without essential reason. Apply it to shift action accidental-activation review.
